content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All five amendments identify verified, real defects in the planned-elsewhere reconciliation subsystem. Two amendments (fr-3-1efa3b60 and fr-3-d9102962) overlap on the same core bug (verification state never persisted) but approach it from different angles—both are valid. The remaining three target distinct issues: incorrect force-completion gating, missing sync durability for comments, and a broken journal-rebuild guarantee. Each was confirmed by reading the relevant source code paths.

## Votes

- `fr-3-1efa3b60`: Accept
  Confirmed: verify_and_comment_mappings returns Vec<MappingVerificationOutcome> but no caller persists verified=true back to the NDJSON sidecar. The PlannedElsewhereMappingPort trait only exposes read_mappings() and append_mapping()—there is no update method. Since success_reconciliation.rs filters on !mapped_bead_verified (line 699-702), every subsequent reconciliation run will re-verify the same mappings and re-post duplicate br comments. This is a concrete idempotency bug with real side effects.
- `fr-3-3bd1a49d`: Accept
  Confirmed: The early return at final_review.rs:1503 checks `!final_accepted_amendments.is_empty() && final_review_restart_count >= max_restarts` without distinguishing planned-elsewhere amendments. The downstream check at line 1551-1553 correctly uses `any(|a| a.mapped_to_bead_id.is_none())` to filter, proving the intent is to only force-complete for regular amendments. The early return lacks this filter, causing unnecessary force_completed=true when all accepted amendments are planned-elsewhere. The proposed fix is consistent with the existing downstream logic.
- `fr-3-46f7f076`: Accept
  Confirmed: The sync at success_reconciliation.rs:209 (sync_after_close) happens BEFORE the planned-elsewhere comment posting at planned_elsewhere.rs:291. comment_bead() sets has_unsync_mutations=true via exec_tracked_mutation, but no sync_flush() is called after the comment posting returns. The reconciliation function completes without a final flush, so comments are in a dirty state that can be lost on process exit. This is a real data loss risk.
- `fr-3-d9102962`: Accept
  Confirmed: This describes the same core defect as fr-3-1efa3b60 (verification never persisted) but correctly identifies the engine-side contribution—engine.rs:7618 records every mapping as mapped_bead_verified:false, and no code path updates this to true in any durable store. The journal-perspective framing adds value since the journal is documented as the authoritative audit record, yet it too never receives verification updates. Both amendments are valid and complementary.
- `fr-3-a1a9ae8d`: Accept
  Confirmed: record_planned_elsewhere_mapping (service.rs:2348-2390) explicitly documents that 'the NDJSON file can be rebuilt from the journal', but load_planned_elsewhere_mappings (service.rs:2395-2401) is a trivial passthrough to mapping_store.read_mappings() with zero journal fallback. The engine (engine.rs:7644-7656) logs sidecar-write failures as warnings and continues. A crash or I/O error between journal append and sidecar write leaves a mapping permanently invisible to reload, directly contradicting the documented recovery guarantee.
